extra.message: User-controlled input `username` from the query string is concatenated directly into a Jinja2 template string passed to `Environment().from_string()`. Because the user input is embedded into the template source itself (not passed as a render variable), an attacker can inject Jinja2 template expressions such as `{{ 7*7 }}` or `{{ config }}` or `{{ ''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__() }}` to achieve Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). With a sufficiently crafted payload this leads to arbitrary Python code execution on the server. The safe implementation is commented out directly below the vulnerable line: `Jinja2.from_string('Welcome {{ user_name }}!').render(user_name=username)`.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
